summary: glob-helper postinstall exfiltrates host info and filesystem contents over HTTP
details: On install, postinstall.js collects host identity via os.hostname() and os.userInfo(), reads files from the user's home directory (os.homedir() + fs.existsSync / fs.readFileSync), and transmits the data via http.request(). The package name suggests a generic glob utility, which has no legitimate need to read home-directory files or beacon system information at install time. The combination of host identity collection, home-directory file reads, and an outbound HTTP request inside a postinstall lifecycle script is a credential/host-exfiltration shape that fires on `npm install` without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
